prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene comparing Transform and ReplacementTransform. Show one shape morphing through two targets, keep a short caption explaining the difference, and make the transition visibly animation-focused rather than static.
completion:
from manim import *

class TransformReplacementComparison(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Transform vs ReplacementTransform", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        source = Circle(radius=0.9, color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.18)
        target_a = Square(side_length=1.8, color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.18)
        target_b = Triangle(color=YELLOW_D, fill_opacity=0.18).scale(1.1)
        label = Text("Transform keeps reusing the same scene object", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)
        followup = Text("ReplacementTransform swaps the old object for the new one", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(source), FadeIn(label), run_time=3)
        self.play(Transform(source, target_a), run_time=2)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(source, target_b), Transform(label, followup), run_time=3)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene where an input token fans out to three feature boxes. Use LaggedStart to reveal the arrows and AnimationGroup to light up the boxes together at the end.
completion:
from manim import *

class LaggedStartAttentionFanout(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Animation Composition for Staged Reveal", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        token = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.1, width=1.6, height=0.75, color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.16).shift(LEFT * 4)
        token_label = Text("token", font_size=22, color=BLUE_D).move_to(token)
        boxes = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(
                RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.1, width=1.8, height=0.75, color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.12),
                Text(label, font_size=21, color=TEAL_D),
            )
            for label in ["shape", "color", "motion"]
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.7).shift(RIGHT * 2.6)
        for group in boxes:
            group[1].move_to(group[0])
        arrows = VGroup(*[Arrow(token.get_right(), group[0].get_left(), buff=0.15, color=GRAY_B) for group in boxes])
        caption = Text("LaggedStart staggers the reveal, then AnimationGroup synchronizes emphasis.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(token), FadeIn(token_label), run_time=2)
        self.play(LaggedStart(*[GrowArrow(arrow) for arrow in arrows], lag_ratio=0.18), LaggedStart(*[FadeIn(group) for group in boxes], lag_ratio=0.18), run_time=4)
        self.play(AnimationGroup(*[group[0].animate.set_fill(YELLOW_D, opacity=0.28) for group in boxes], lag_ratio=0.0), FadeIn(caption), run_time=2)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene showing a token moving through three pipeline stages. Use Succession so the token moves and each stage is indicated in sequence.
completion:
from manim import *

class SuccessionPipelineToken(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("Succession Plays One Animation After Another", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        stages = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(
                RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.1, width=1.8, height=0.85, color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.12),
                Text(label, font_size=22, color=TEAL_D),
            )
            for label in ["parse", "plan", "animate"]
        ]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=1.0).shift(UP * 0.2)
        for group in stages:
            group[1].move_to(group[0])
        token = Dot(color=YELLOW, radius=0.1).move_to(stages[0][0].get_left() + RIGHT * 0.3)
        paths = [
            Line(token.get_center(), stages[0][0].get_center()),
            Line(stages[0][0].get_center(), stages[1][0].get_center()),
            Line(stages[1][0].get_center(), stages[2][0].get_center()),
        ]
        connectors = VGroup(*[Arrow(stages[i][0].get_right(), stages[i + 1][0].get_left(), buff=0.15, color=GRAY_B) for i in range(2)])
        caption = Text("Succession is useful when the order itself is the story.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), FadeIn(stages), FadeIn(connectors), FadeIn(token), run_time=3)
        self.play(
            Succession(
                MoveAlongPath(token, paths[0], rate_func=linear),
                Indicate(stages[0][0], color=YELLOW_D),
                MoveAlongPath(token, paths[1], rate_func=linear),
                Indicate(stages[1][0], color=YELLOW_D),
                MoveAlongPath(token, paths[2], rate_func=linear),
                Indicate(stages[2][0], color=YELLOW_D),
            ),
            FadeIn(caption),
            run_time=5,
        )
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a short Manim scene that morphs one phrase into another using TransformMatchingShapes, so the animation emphasizes shared letter geometry rather than a simple fade.
completion:
from manim import *

class MatchingShapesWordMorph(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        source = Text("signal flows", font_size=56)
        target = Text("flows signal", font_size=56)
        caption = Text("Matching subshapes gives a much cleaner text morph.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(Write(source), run_time=2)
        self.play(TransformMatchingShapes(source, target, path_arc=PI / 2), FadeIn(caption), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene where a dot travels along a curved path while a live-updating line stays attached to it. Make the scene clearly about the motion animation itself.
completion:
from manim import *

class MoveAlongPathWithLink(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("MoveAlongPath with an Updater", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        dot = Dot(LEFT * 4 + DOWN * 1.3, color=ORANGE, radius=0.1)
        path = VMobject(color=BLUE_D)
        path.set_points_smoothly([LEFT * 4 + DOWN * 1.3, LEFT * 1.5 + UP * 1.0, RIGHT * 1.0 + DOWN * 0.4, RIGHT * 4 + UP * 1.2])
        tether = VMobject(color=YELLOW_D)
        tether.add_updater(lambda mob: mob.become(Line(LEFT * 5, dot.get_center(), color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=5)))
        caption = Text("The updater reacts every frame while the path animation runs.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(path), FadeIn(dot), run_time=3)
        self.add(tether)
        self.play(MoveAlongPath(dot, path), FadeIn(caption), rate_func=linear, run_time=5)
        self.wait(2)

prompt: Create a 10-second Manim scene that demonstrates changing animation speed over time. Use ChangeSpeed on a moving dot and keep another shape spinning continuously so the timing difference is obvious.
completion:
from manim import *

class SpeedModifierWithSpinner(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        title = Text("ChangeSpeed Re-times an Animation", font_size=34).to_edge(UP)
        dot = Dot(LEFT * 4 + DOWN * 1.1, color=YELLOW, radius=0.1)
        tracer = TracedPath(dot.get_center, stroke_color=YELLOW_D, stroke_width=4)
        spinner = Triangle(color=TEAL_D, fill_opacity=0.18).scale(0.6).shift(UP * 1.2)
        always_rotate(spinner, rate=2 * PI, about_point=spinner.get_center())
        baseline = Line(LEFT * 4 + DOWN * 1.1, RIGHT * 4 + DOWN * 1.1, color=GRAY_B)
        caption = Text("The dot changes speed, but the triangle keeps rotating steadily.", font_size=24, color=GRAY_D).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.add(tracer)
        self.play(FadeIn(title), Create(baseline), FadeIn(dot), FadeIn(spinner), run_time=2)
        self.play(
            ChangeSpeed(
                dot.animate.shift(RIGHT * 8),
                speedinfo={0.0: 0.5, 0.3: 1.8, 0.65: 0.4, 1.0: 1.0},
                rate_func=linear,
            ),
            FadeIn(caption),
            run_time=5,
        )
        self.wait(3)
